channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Regional director appointed hospital CEO
item.description: A hospital trust has appointed a long-serving medical leader – who is currently regional medical director – as its chief executive.

item.title: Capital approvals still ‘very, very elongated’, says CFO
item.description: Approval processes for NHS capital spending remain “very, very elongated” – despite promised improvements – an experienced chief finance officer has told MPs.

item.title: Call for dedicated maternity IT funding
item.description: The Royal College of Midwives has called for ring-fenced funding for dedicated, interoperable IT systems in maternity and neonatal care following the findings of the Amos Review.

item.title: Performance dips after trusts earn bonus cash
item.description: Most trusts that earned capital funding for improved A&E waiting times in March saw their performance deteriorate in the months after, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Government picks CQC chair without competition
item.description: The government has revealed its preferred candidate to be the next chair of the Care Quality Commission, as a cross-bench peer who has overseen major national healthcare reviews.

item.title: New role for ‘culture reset’ chief
item.description: A North West trust has announced a new CEO after the retirement of its current boss.

item.title: Mackey issues 10-point ‘urgent’ maternity plan
item.description: The CEO of NHS England has ordered trust boards to enforce joint accountability for maternity between medical directors and chief nursing officers, following criticisms of “siloed” leadership in major reviews.

item.title: ‘Normal birth’ harms should not be ‘airbrushed away’, warns expert
item.description: An expert who resigned from the national maternity review in a dispute over its stance on “normal birth” ideology has warned that misuse of such an approach and its impact on patient safety “should not be airbrushed away”.

item.title: UK AI tool first to get top safety certification
item.description: A UK-based ambient scribe firm has become the first in the country to achieve UKCA Class IIa certification, a higher form of regulatory accreditation under the UK Conformity Assessment scheme.

item.title: Dash: Primary care has twice the space it needs
item.description: The NHS may have “twice as much” floor space for GP practices and associated services as it actually needs, the chair of NHS England has said.

item.title: Dispute flares up over health centre development
item.description: An integrated care board is in dispute with one of its primary care networks over a bid to convert a former police station into a health centre.

item.title: Contractor assessed its own service in ICB procurement
item.description: An integrated care board has been criticised by a watchdog over poor record-keeping and errors in the decision to award a £2.4m contract.

item.title: New maternity inspection ‘unit’ demanded by government review
item.description: Ministers should create a new specialist unit to assess maternity services because the Care Quality Commission does not have the credibility to do so, a government review has concluded.

item.title: Resident doctors accept pay offer
item.description: Resident doctors in England have voted to accept an offer from the government following long-running industrial action.

item.title: Exclusive: Maternity adviser quits in ‘normal birth’ dispute
item.description: The chair of several high-profile safety inquiries has resigned from the government’s national maternity review just ahead of publication, in a dispute over “normal birth ideology”, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: ICB boss to lead regional team
item.description: An NHS England regional director is to step down after just over a year in the role, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Trust takes £2m hit on flawed land sale
item.description: A trust was forced to pay £2m to exit a land sale – after realising that it might need the space and would have lost money on the deal.

item.title: Exclusive: Regulators poised to strip back AI rules
item.description: Regulators are about to significantly strip back regulation of ambient voice technology – one of the fastest-growing healthcare AI tools – HSJ has learned.

item.title: NHS manager died after being ‘lost to follow up’
item.description: An NHS manager died after an urgent referral was “recategorised” and a triage time of six weeks was arranged instead.

item.title: Revealed: Promised ‘neighbourhood health centres’ already met requirements
item.description: Nearly all the first wave of “neighbourhood health centres” – currently being developed for launch by next year – were already doing the job required of the model, government documents reveal.
